module_mood_contract: practitioner fluency — 'I know how to test, I know how to learn, I know how to encode.' Not confidence-as-performance; competence-as-posture.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room outside text. No authored conversation cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 44: 'The trainer reads the room and picks the form (pair exchange, whole-room round, quiet write-then-share, or skip the round when energy is spent).' — Cohort-management direction (trainer cue, form-picking for the room) authored in the student-facing body. Canonical banned shape: naming the form-picker ('the trainer') and listing delivery options inside body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Move the form-picking options to the maintainer block. Body should read only what the student does: articulate one key learning and one personal thought on the future.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-exercise leap test — name three observable Monday-morning outcomes in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module maintainer block has no leap-test section. Exercise maintainer block (exercises/spot-gaps-build-the-loop.md line 171-174) has a TODO noting the three-persona sim is pending but no three observable Monday-morning outcomes. The story judge is the blocking gate for missing or unfalsifiable leap tests per rule 45.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a 'Leap test' section to the module maintainer block naming three concrete, falsifiable Monday-morning outcomes (e.g., 'opens the M5 worktree and diffs the two branches without being prompted', 'authors a second skill via conversation not editor', 'ships the skill to ~/.claude/skills/ and verifies auto-discovery').

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Per-phase failure mode + escape hatch — every forcing function in maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module maintainer block has push-back moves and watch-fors but no 'Failure modes + escape hatches per forcing function' structure keyed to specific phase forcing functions. The exercise file (exercises/spot-gaps-build-the-loop.md lines 142-168) has failure modes listed, but the module maintainer block (which is the locus for rule 47 for the module file) does not mirror a structured per-forcing-function row with dominant failure + one escape hatch move. Rule 47 requires the maintainer block of the file shipping the forcing function to carry this.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a structured 'Forcing function failure modes' section to the module maintainer block — one row per load-bearing forcing function (Phase 1 quote-requirement, Phase 2 author-through-conversation, Phase 2 self-critique, Phase 2 invoke-before-ship), each naming (a) dominant failure, (b) one escape hatch (trainer move, Nerd line, or fallback prompt).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
Strategy-fidelity check — no front-running of downstream teaching moments.
Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts.
Key Concepts shape — theme breadcrumbs, not standalone section.
Meta-frame lectures are closers; demo/ceiling lectures are openers.

rules_evaluated.evidence: trace.phases[0].mood_score = 7. The 'Human close' section authored a trainer cue ('the trainer reads the room') inside the student-facing body, breaking the self-study persona's sense of being addressed. The mood recovers through the exercise and Key Concepts but the close beat stalls at 7 rather than landing at 8.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Removing the trainer-cue sentence from student body (rule 27 fix) should pull this beat to 8. The self-study variant line alone is sufficient for solo readers.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood does not resolve early — M6 should not pre-empt M7.
Teaching moment lands — reliably triggers across persona-skill variation.
Big Idea fidelity — module leads with or honours strategy doc's Big Idea.
Module-to-module arc — close hands off to M7 open.

stealing_the_mood: Human close section (lines 43-48): trainer-cue sentence ('the trainer reads the room and picks the form') breaks the self-study reader's sense of being addressed — register gap stalls the practitioner-fluency landing at 7.
